title: Perform a Static Engine Test
description: Run a controlled static burn, record measurements, and only clear flight when thrust data passes bounds.

dialogue.text: Before launch day, let's run a static burn. We'll gather baseline data, apply pass/fail bounds, and only proceed if the curve is sane.

options.text: Let's secure the stand and instrumentation.

dialogue.text: Clamp the rocket to a test stand, set a remote ignition line, and establish a 5-meter safety perimeter.

options.text: Stand is locked and perimeter is clear.
The setup feels sketchy. Hold the test.

dialogue.text: Record baseline: ambient wind, expected burn duration, and target peak thrust window before ignition.
Ignite remotely and capture the thrust curve. Keep observers behind cover until burnout and cooldown are complete.

options.text: Ignition behavior was unstable. Abort and investigate.

dialogue.text: Interpretation gate: pass only when the curve is smooth, peak thrust sits in target bounds, and no pressure spike appears.

options.text: Data passes bounds and is safe for launch planning.
Data is out of range. We need a corrective loop.

dialogue.text: Corrective loop: inspect nozzle alignment, retighten mount hardware, and replace any suspect igniter leads before re-test.

options.text: Corrections made. Re-log baseline and run another test.

dialogue.text: Safety hold accepted. Keep the motor cold, clear the pad, and resume only after hazards are removed.

options.text: Hazards cleared. Rebuild the test setup.

dialogue.text: Great work. You validated the motor with measurable evidence and a disciplined stop/retest path.
